sources.sourceName: Introduction to Sociology, OpenStax 3e (Pressbooks adaptation) - Appendix: Chapter Quiz Answers (odd-numbered)

meta.notes: Sociology (PSBB) items scraped verbatim from OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e Ch. 10 and Ch. 12 section-quiz pages; correct answers from the OpenStax-derived odd-numbered answer key (Pressbooks CC BY 4.0 adaptation). From Ch. 12, two True/False items (Q7, Q12) were excluded because they cannot be reformatted into four unambiguous MCAT choices, and Q17 (most-supported sex-ed program) was excluded because its keyed answer conflicts with what national survey research supports (avoiding a possibly-wrong answer). Live OpenStax page shows CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 plus a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing/non-AI per PRD.

questions.stem: A sociologist who focuses on the way that multinational corporations headquartered in core nations exploit the local workers in their peripheral nation factories is using a _________ perspective to understand the global economy.
questions.explanation: Focusing on exploitation and inequality between powerful and less powerful groups reflects the conflict theory perspective.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q1 = B.

questions.stem: A sociologist working from a symbolic interaction perspective would:

choices.label: A
choices.text: study how inequality is created and reproduced

choices.label: B
choices.text: study how corporations can improve the lives of their low-income workers

choices.label: C
choices.text: try to understand how companies provide an advantage to high-income nations compared to low-income nations

choices.label: D
choices.text: want to interview women working in factories to understand how they manage the expectations of their supervisors, make ends meet, and support their households on a day-to-day basis

questions.explanation: Symbolic interactionism works at the micro level, so a researcher would interview individuals to understand their day-to-day experiences and meanings.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q3 = D.

questions.stem: In the past, the United States manufactured clothes. Many clothing corporations have shut down their U.S. factories and relocated to China. This is an example of:
questions.explanation: Capital flight is the movement of capital (factories, investment, jobs) from one location to another, such as relocating clothing production from the U.S. to China.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q5 = D.

questions.stem: Maya is a twelve-year-old girl living in Thailand. She is homeless, and often does not know where she will sleep or when she will eat. We might say that Maya lives in _________ poverty.
questions.explanation: Absolute poverty is the inability to secure basic necessities like food and shelter, as in Maya's situation.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q7 = B.

questions.stem: Faith has a full-time job and two children. She has enough money for the basics and can pay her rent each month, but she feels that, with her education and experience, her income should be enough for her family to live much better than they do. Faith is experiencing:
questions.explanation: Subjective poverty is based on a person's perception that their resources are inadequate relative to their expectations, as with Faith's sense that she should be better off.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q9 = B.

questions.stem: One flaw in dependency theory is the unwillingness to recognize _______.

choices.label: A
choices.text: that previously low-income nations such as China have successfully developed their economies and can no longer be classified as dependent on core nations

choices.label: B
choices.text: that previously high-income nations such as China have been economically overpowered by low-income nations entering the global marketplace

choices.label: C
choices.text: that countries such as China are growing more dependent on core nations

choices.label: D
choices.text: that countries such as China do not necessarily want to be more like core nations

questions.explanation: Dependency theory struggles to explain cases like China, a once low-income nation that successfully developed and is no longer dependent on core nations.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q11 = A.

questions.stem: If a sociologist says that nations evolve toward more advanced technology and more complex industry as their citizens learn cultural values that celebrate hard work and success, she is using _______ theory to study the global economy.
questions.explanation: Modernization theory argues that nations develop as they adopt cultural values and technologies associated with industrialization and achievement.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q13 = A.

questions.stem: Dependency theorists explain global inequality and global stratification by focusing on the way that:

choices.label: A
choices.text: core nations and peripheral nations exploit semi-peripheral nations

questions.explanation: Dependency theory holds that global inequality results from core (wealthy) nations exploiting peripheral (poor) nations.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 10 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 10 Q15 = D.

questions.stem: The terms "masculine" and "feminine" refer to a person's _________.
questions.explanation: Masculine and feminine describe gender (socially constructed roles and identities), as distinguished from biological sex.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 12 Section Quiz

questions.stem: Research indicates that individuals are aware of their sexual orientation _______.
questions.explanation: Per the source, research indicates that people typically become aware of their sexual orientation in early adolescence.

questions.stem: Which of the following is the best example of the role peers play as an agent of socialization for school-aged children?

choices.label: A
choices.text: Children can act however they wish around their peers because children are unaware of gender roles.

choices.label: B
choices.text: Peers serve as a support system for children who wish to act outside of their assigned gender roles.

choices.label: C
choices.text: Peers tend to reinforce gender roles by criticizing and marginalizing those who behave outside of their assigned roles.

questions.explanation: Peers are powerful agents of gender socialization, often reinforcing gender roles by criticizing or marginalizing children who deviate from them.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 12 Section Quiz
questions.sourceLicense: CC BY-NC-SA 4.0 (OpenStax also posts a no-LLM-training notice; SpeedyCAT use is human-facing, non-AI per PRD)
questions.notes: Question + options scraped verbatim from OpenStax. correctAnswer from the OpenStax-derived chapter quiz answer key (Pressbooks adaptation, CC BY 4.0), Ch. 12 Q5 = C.

choices.label: A
choices.text: A certain kind if spa treatment

choices.label: C
choices.text: How we know our gender roles

choices.label: D
choices.text: the hatred of or, aversion to, or prejudice against women

questions.explanation: Misogyny is the hatred of, aversion to, or prejudice against women.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 12 Section Quiz

questions.stem: The idea that gender inequality comes from the division of labor fits with which Sociological theory?
questions.explanation: Per the source, the view that gender inequality arises from the division of labor is keyed to conflict theory, which emphasizes how divisions of labor produce unequal power and resources.
questions.sourceName: OpenStax Introduction to Sociology 3e, Ch. 12 Section Quiz

questions.stem: In the 115th Congress of the United States, what percentage of the elected officials were women?
questions.explanation: Per the source, women made up about 21.2% of the elected officials in the 115th U.S. Congress, illustrating the underrepresentation of women in political institutions.

questions.stem: Compared to most Western societies, U.S. sexual attitudes are considered _______.
questions.explanation: Compared with most other Western societies, U.S. sexual attitudes are relatively conservative.
